title: Pre-Modern Local Population Counts (Tehran 1868, Shiraz 1883-84, Kerman 1874)
notes: Pre-national-census local counts (first modern-style census attempt in Persia, city-level household/population counts). No national census existed yet; nothing in WDI/Maddison predates 1868 for Iran at this granularity. Genuinely new micro-historical data points.

title: Iran National Census Population Totals (1956-1996, Encyclopaedia Iranica)
notes: Headline population totals at each of the 8 national censuses (1956-1996) plus 1995 mid-decade estimate. Cross-validates to the exact digit against un-demographic-yearbook-iran and wb_archives_population_demographic_citations_1956_1982.csv (1956: 18,954,704; 1976: 33,708,744; 1986: 49,445,010 -- three independent sources agree exactly per this folder's own README). WDI's SP.POP.TOTL for Iran only starts 1960, so 1956 census total is a genuine pre-WDI depth extension; later years are corroborating alt-source detail (census methodology, staff counts, under-enumeration rates) not carried by WDI at all.

title: Urban Share of Population by Census Year (1956-1986, Encyclopaedia Iranica)
notes: Urban share at each census (1956: 31%, 1966: 38%, 1976: 47%, 1986: 54.3% -- note 1986 figure flagged in source as administrative-definition change, not directly comparable to prior censuses). WDI's SP.URB.TOTL.IN.ZS starts 1960, so the 1956 point is a genuine pre-WDI extension.

title: Life Expectancy at Birth, Narrative Estimates (1959, 1990)
notes: Only two data points (46 yrs in 1959, 62 yrs in 1990). 1959 predates WDI's SP.DYN.LE00.IN coverage (starts 1960) by one year; 1990 duplicates WDI's own coverage. Thin series, kept as a citation-level extension rather than a standalone chart of real weight.

title: Fertility, Family Planning & Contraceptive Distribution (1956-1991)
underlying_codes: iran_census_demographics_series/iranica_census_demography_narrative_1868_1998.csv (metrics: fertility rate, children born per woman, birth-control pill distribution, abortion share, family-planning acceptance)
notes: Children-born-per-woman at 1986/1991 census overlaps conceptually with wdi__SP.DYN.TFRT (which also covers those years for Iran), but the public/private-sector contraceptive-pill distribution volumes, pre-abortion-share-of-births statistic, and 1978 family-planning-acceptance survey figure have no WDI equivalent at all -- kept as one new bundled chart concept rather than force-fitting only the fertility-rate sub-points onto wdi__SP.DYN.TFRT.

title: Infant & General Mortality Rates, Urban/Rural Split (1955-1976)
underlying_codes: iran_census_demographics_series/iranica_census_demography_narrative_1868_1998.csv (Population Growth Survey rural/urban IMR/mortality rows) + iran_census_demographics_series/wb_archives_population_demographic_citations_1956_1982.csv (PBO-series IMR rows, 1955/56-1972)
notes: Two independently-sourced mortality series covering complementary windows: Iranica's 1973-1976 Population Growth Survey rural/urban infant & general mortality split, and the World Bank archives' 1955/56-1972 PBO-series infant mortality point estimates. WDI's SP.DYN.IMRT.IN for Iran starts 1971 and carries no urban/rural split -- both citation sources add pre-1971 depth and a residence breakdown WDI never has for Iran.

title: Marriage Age Gap, Internal Migration & Post-Revolution Exile Population (1956-1991)
underlying_codes: iran_census_demographics_series/iranica_census_demography_narrative_1868_1998.csv (metrics: marriage age gap, share of women 10-49 married, non-migrant shares, Tehran migration destination share, exile population)
notes: No WDI equivalent for any of these (age-at-first-marriage gap, birthplace-residence non-migrant share, Tehran as migration destination, ~3 million 1978-79-revolution/war-era exile estimate). The marriage-age-gap metric is thematically close to this project's own iran_disasters_regional_series/mean_age_at_first_marriage_1966_2006.csv (staged separately in this same batch, see that row) -- not set as extends_chart_id since that sibling file is itself only staged, not yet in CHART_REGISTRY.csv, but flagged here for a future merge pass.

title: Nomadic Population Counts & Share of Rural Population (c.1900-1990s)
underlying_codes: iran_census_demographics_series/iranica_census_demography_narrative_1868_1998.csv (metrics: nomadic population, nomadic share of rural population)
notes: 1987 dedicated nomad census (1,152,099 persons) vs. 1976 general-census undercount (337,176) vs. c.1900 and 1990s share-of-rural-population comparison points. No WDI indicator tracks nomadic/tribal population for Iran. Thematically adjacent to data/processed/nomadic_pastoral_economy/ (out of this task's scope, already has its own README per the census folder's README) -- cross-reference noted, not merged.

title: Crude Birth & Death Rates, Two Independent WB-Cited Series (1956-1972)
underlying_codes: iran_census_demographics_series/wb_archives_population_demographic_citations_1956_1982.csv (metrics: crude birth rate, crude death rate, incl. both the 'demographic estimate' series and the separate PBO series)
notes: Two parallel citation series in the same WB source (a 1971-report 'demographic estimate' series and a distinct Plan & Budget Organization intercensal series) for the same crude birth/death rate concept -- both preserved as printed rather than reconciled, per this project's raw-data-immutability convention. WDI's SP.DYN.CBRT.IN/SP.DYN.CDRT.IN for Iran start 1960; the 1956/1958 points here predate that.

title: Gross/Net Reproduction Rate & Completed Family Size, Rural vs. Tehran (1966-1982)
underlying_codes: iran_census_demographics_series/wb_archives_population_demographic_citations_1956_1982.csv (metrics: gross/net reproduction rate, completed family size, incl. two conflicting 1966 life-expectancy estimates flagged in the source itself)
notes: Net/gross reproduction rate and completed-family-size-per-married-woman (rural vs. Tehran split) have no WDI equivalent for Iran. The row also carries the source's own internally-conflicting 1966 life-expectancy estimates (53 vs. 45.6 years, both flagged 'NOTE conflicts' in the raw extraction) and 1971-vintage Plan Organization 1982 projections -- kept together as one coherent 'reproductive demography' chart rather than split further.

title: Tehran City Population, Household Size & National Primacy Share (1956-1972)
notes: City-level data for Tehran specifically (population, annual growth rate 6.6%/yr 1956-1966, national population share, households, persons/household, city-size-tier breakdown of the urban hierarchy). WDI has no sub-national/city-level population series for Iran at all -- this is a genuinely new granularity, not an extension of the national urbanization chart.

title: Economically Active Population & Labor Force Participation by Sex (1956-1972)
underlying_codes: iran_census_demographics_series/wb_archives_population_demographic_citations_1956_1982.csv (metrics: active population by sex, labor force participation rate by sex, unemployment rate by sex)
notes: Male/female labor force participation rate (age 10+) and unemployment rate at 1956 and 1966 censuses plus a 1972 Plan Organization projection. WDI's SL.TLF.CACT family for Iran only starts 1990 -- this adds 34 years of pre-WDI depth. Complements (does not duplicate) iran_disasters_regional_series/employed_population_by_gender_1966_2011.csv (staged separately below), which starts a decade later at 1966; this row's 1956 data point is the earliest labor-force-by-sex figure in the whole project. The 1956/1966 sectoral (agriculture/industry/services) breakdown recorded alongside these same rows is NOT separately registered here -- it duplicates, at coarser 2-year resolution, the fuller 1956-2011 annual series staged as iran_disasters__employed_persons_by_sector_1956_2011 below; noted there as a cross-validation source instead of a standalone chart.

title: Population & Average Annual Growth Rate, National + by Province (2006 vs. 2011 Census)
underlying_codes: iran_census_demographics_series/national_and_provincial_summary_2011_census.csv (Table 1 - Population Changes; Table 9 - Population & Growth by Province)
notes: National 2006/2011 population + growth rate (Table 1) and the same broken out for all 31 provinces (Table 9). WDI's SP.POP.TOTL has no provincial breakdown at all for Iran -- province-level granularity is the genuine value-add here; the national totals themselves duplicate WDI's existing 2006/2011 coverage.

title: Household Size & Sex Share by Urban/Rural Residence (2011 Census)
notes: Average household size and male/female share of population split by urban/rural residence. WDI carries no household-size indicator for Iran at all.

title: Population by Religion (2011 Census)
notes: Muslim/Christian/Jewish/Zoroastrian/other/undeclared counts, the only religion-disaggregated population breakdown anywhere in this project. No WDI or FAOSTAT equivalent exists.

title: Population by Citizenship/Nationality (Iranian vs. Afghan/Iraqi/Pakistani/Turkish/Other), 2006-2016
underlying_codes: iran_census_demographics_series/national_and_provincial_summary_2011_census.csv (Table 4 - Population by Citizenship) + national_summary_2016_census.csv (Population by nationality, 2006-2016 table)
notes: Two census bulletins' citizenship/nationality breakdown combined into one chart concept (same underlying question, 2011 cross-section + 2006-2016 trend). Distinct from wdi__SM.POP (net migration / migrant stock, a single aggregate flow number) -- this gives the actual resident-population breakdown by nationality, which WDI does not carry for Iran.

title: Population by 5-Year Age Group, National Cross-Section (2011 Census)
notes: 18 five-year age bands, both-sexes total, single cross-section. WDI carries the same concept as many separate single-band indicators (SP.POP.0004.MA/FE.5Y etc., each its own chart_id) -- no single WDI chart_id bundles the full age pyramid the way this census table does, so this is registered as one extension of the broad population chart rather than 18 separate rows.

title: Housing Tenure Type, National (2006-2016)
underlying_codes: iran_census_demographics_series/national_and_provincial_summary_2011_census.csv (Table 6 - Housing Tenure Type) + national_summary_2016_census.csv (Household tenure type, 2006-2016 table)
notes: Owned/rented/free-of-charge/in-lieu-of-services/other tenure shares, combined across both census bulletins into one chart concept. No WDI housing-tenure indicator exists for Iran.

title: Population Density by Province (2011 Census)
notes: Land area and population density (persons/km2) for all 31 provinces. WDI's EN.POP.DNST is a single national figure -- province-level density is new granularity extending that concept.

title: Sex Ratio (Males per 100 Females) by Province (2011 Census)
notes: General population sex ratio by province. Distinct from wdi__SP.POP.BRTH (sex ratio AT BIRTH, a different demographic concept) -- no WDI indicator gives general population sex ratio by province for Iran.

title: Urbanization Rate by Province (2011 Census)
notes: Province-level urbanization rate. WDI's SP.URB.TOTL.IN.ZS is national only -- province breakdown is the value-add.

title: Average Household Size by Province (2011 Census)
notes: Province-level average household size, 31 provinces. No WDI equivalent (WDI has no household-size indicator for Iran at any granularity).

title: Population, Household & Sex-Ratio Trends, National (1986-2016) + Increase by Intercensal Period
underlying_codes: iran_census_demographics_series/national_summary_2016_census.csv (Population & household trends 1986-2016 table; Population increase by period 1956-2016 table)
notes: National population/household/sex-ratio trend across 5 census years, plus absolute and annualized population increase for each intercensal period back to 1956. WDI's SP.POP.TOTL already covers 1960 onward at national level; the households/sex-ratio companion series and the pre-1960-anchored 1956 intercensal-increase figure are the genuine additions.

title: Households & Average Household Size by Urban/Rural Residence (1976-2016)
notes: Longitudinal urban/rural household-count and household-size trend. No WDI household-size indicator exists for Iran.

title: Population Age Structure, Broad Groups (0-14 / 15-29 / 30-64 / 65+), 1986-2016
notes: % share in each of 4 broad age bands across 5 census years. Closely parallels WDI's broad-band age-share indicators (SP.POP.0014.TO.ZS, SP.POP.1564.TO.ZS, SP.POP.65UP.TO.ZS -- WDI splits 15-64 as one band, this table splits it into 15-29/30-64), which WDI already covers annually for these years; the value-add is the census-anchored cross-check at exact census years plus the 15-29/30-64 sub-split WDI doesn't carry.

title: Mean & Median Age of Population by Urban/Rural Residence (2006-2016)
underlying_codes: iran_census_demographics_series/national_summary_2016_census.csv (Mean age of population by residence table; Median age of population by residence table)
notes: No WDI mean-age or median-age indicator exists for Iran at all (WDI carries age-band shares, not a single summary mean/median-age statistic), let alone split by residence.

title: Population Aged 10+ by Marital Status and Sex (2006-2016)
notes: Married/never-married/widowed/divorced/unstated shares by sex across 3 census years. No WDI marital-status indicator exists for Iran.

title: Literacy Rate by Province and by Age Cohort (6+ and 10-49), 2006-2016
underlying_codes: iran_census_demographics_series/national_summary_2016_census.csv (Literacy rate (age 6+) by province, 2006-2016 table; Literacy rate (age 10-49), 2006-2016 table)
notes: WDI's SE.ADT.LITR.ZS for Iran runs 1976-2023 at national level only -- these census tables add province-level detail (absent from WDI entirely) and an age-10-49 cohort variant, both new granularity even though the national headline years overlap WDI's existing coverage.

title: Average Annual Population Growth Rate by Province (2011-2016)
underlying_codes: iran_census_demographics_series/national_summary_2016_census.csv (Average annual growth rate by province, 2011-2016 table)
notes: Province-level growth rate for the 2011-2016 intercensal period, complementing the iran_census__population_growth_national_province_2006_2011 row above (2006-2011 period) with the following intercensal window from the same bulletin series.

title: Provincial Population, 1956 & 1966 Censuses + 1967-1972 Intercensal Estimates
notes: Province/governorate-level population, reshaped wide-to-long from WB 1974 Statistical Appendix Table 1.1. WDI has no province breakdown ever for Iran, and the 1956/1966/1967 points predate WDI's 1960 start -- genuine pre-WDI, sub-national depth extension. Source footnote: province totals don't sum exactly to the national total (independent province-by-province projection basis), preserved as printed.

title: Population by Province x 5-Year Age Group x Sex x Residence (2011 Census)
notes: 7,548-row full cross-tabulation (31 provinces x age bands x sex x Total/Urban/Rural/Unsettled residence), extracted from 31 separate provincial source files. Sums reconcile exactly to the national 75,149,669 total. This is the single most granular demographic cross-section in the whole project; WDI has no province-level data for Iran at any granularity, so this is a major extension of the population chart family rather than any single existing WDI chart_id.

title: Population by Province x 5-Year Age Group x Sex (2016 Census)
notes: 1,440-row province x age-band x sex cross-tabulation, the 2016-census companion to the 2011 file above (no residence/urban-rural split at this vintage). Includes the corrected Kerman/Kermanshah mislabeling fix documented in this folder's README (raw source itself labeled both provinces' rows 'Kermanshah'; fixed on the way in per bookkeeping.md, raw file left untouched).

title: Population & Households by Province, incl. Urban/Rural/Unsettled Split (2016 Census)
notes: National + 31-province population, households, sex split, and urban/settled-rural/unsettled breakdown. Sums reconcile exactly to national totals (79,926,270 persons; 24,196,035 households). Deliberately excludes ~1,800 rows of shahrestan (sub-province) detail present in the raw file (kept for a future deeper pass, per README).

title: Household Expenditure Composition, % Shares by Category, Urban & Rural (1965-1971)
notes: World Bank 1974 Statistical Appendix Table 9.2: food/tobacco, housing, clothing, education, recreation, etc. as % of total household expenditure, urban vs. rural, plus households-surveyed sample sizes. No WDI, FAOSTAT, or WID.world equivalent -- pre-HEIS-microdata-era household budget survey detail unique to this project.

title: Household Expenditure Levels, Rials/Month/Household by Category (1965-1971)
notes: Companion table to the shares file above (WB 1974 Table 9.1), same survey, absolute rial values instead of %. Note: 1965-1967 urban figures are Bank Markazi Iran (BMI) data rather than SCI data, a methodological break flagged in the source's own footnote and carried into the file's notes column.

title: Households by Monthly Expenditure Bracket, Urban/Rural/Total (1971)
notes: % of households in each expenditure bracket (Rls <2,000 to 30,000+), a household-level inequality proxy. Distinct from data/processed/inequality_wid_world.csv's top-income-share series per this folder's own README (different unit of analysis: household expenditure brackets vs. individual/adult pre-tax income shares) -- not an extension of any wid__ chart_id. Source itself labels this 'Mission estimates' (WB mission's own tabulation from SCI survey microdata, once removed from a directly-published SCI table).

title: Dairy Product Consumption (High/Low-Value Split, Fat-Equivalent Basis) & Livestock Supply (1972, 1977p)
notes: High-value (fresh milk, urban yogurt, cream, powdered milk) vs. low-value (rural yogurt, cheese, butter, butter-oil) dairy products on a common fat-equivalent basis, plus livestock head counts (cow/goat/sheep/buffalo) on the supply side. Loosely related to faostat__Milk - Excluding Butter__consumption and 'faostat__Butter, Ghee__consumption' but not set as extends_chart_id -- FAOSTAT has no fat-equivalent unit conversion, no high/low-value product split, and no supply-side livestock-head data bundled with consumption the way this WB table does; a genuinely different chart structure, not a unit-variant of the same series. 1977 values are the source's own 1974-report projections, not realized outcomes (see file's own notes column) -- do not chart as historical fact.

title: Food Demand, Actual (1972) & Projected Low/High Scenarios (1977, 1982), by Commodity
notes: Rural/urban/total demand for wheat, red/white meat, rice, sugar, vegetable oil, eggs, pulses, dairy, and feedgrains. Only the 1972 column is actual demand; 1977/1982 are explicit 1974-vintage World Bank mission forecasts under stated expenditure/population-growth assumptions (recorded per-row) -- the chart concept itself (a demand-projection SCENARIO table, not an observed-outcome series) is what's new, distinct from FAOSTAT's per-commodity actual-consumption charts even though the same commodities appear in both; not registered as 'extends' any single faostat__*__consumption chart_id since the scenario/forecast structure doesn't map onto a continuation of an actual observed time series. Directly complements FAOSTAT FBS (starts 1961, no Iran-specific demand-projection scenarios).

title: Wheat Per-Capita Consumption Index (1954/5=100), Solar Years 1955/56-1957/58
notes: Earliest per-capita wheat consumption figure in this project, 3-6 years before FAOSTAT FBSH starts (1961). Index number (1954/5=100), not an absolute quantity -- unit differs from FAOSTAT's kg/capita/yr, but the underlying question (per-capita wheat consumption trend) is the same chart concept, so registered as a pre-1961 depth extension rather than a new chart.

title: Sugar Per-Capita Consumption Index (1954/5=100), Solar Years 1955/56-1957/58
notes: Same index methodology as the wheat row above, applied to sugar. Matched to the general 'Sugar & Sweeteners' FAOSTAT consumption chart as the closest concept -- FAOSTAT actually carries four separate sugar-related consumption chart_ids (Sugar & Sweeteners / Sugar (Raw Equivalent) / Sugar Crops / Sugar cane); this pre-1961 index doesn't specify refined vs. raw vs. crop-equivalent, so the match is approximate -- flagged for a future agent to refine if the specific FAOSTAT sugar sub-chart matters.

title: Tea Per-Capita Consumption Index (1954/5=100), Solar Years 1955/56-1957/58
notes: Same index methodology as the wheat row above, applied to tea.

title: Non-Food & Aggregate Per-Capita Consumption Index (Tobacco, Textiles, Cement, Electricity, Kerosene, Agricultural Products), 1955-1958
underlying_codes: pahlavi_household_consumption_series/per_capita_consumption_index_1955_1958.csv (item in {tobacco, textiles, cement, electricity, kerosene, agricultural_products})
notes: Remaining 6 items from the same 1955-1958 index table that have no FAOSTAT food-consumption counterpart (tobacco is non-food; textiles/cement/electricity/kerosene are industrial & energy usage indices; 'agricultural_products' is an aggregate index, not a single commodity). Bundled as one chart-registry row since they share the same source table and index methodology (1954/5=100) rather than being genuinely separable time series in their own right at this sparse 3-year depth.

title: Significant Earthquakes: Location, Magnitude, Deaths, Injuries, Housing & Economic Damage (1956-2023)
notes: 36 quantified-damage events from NOAA NCEI's Significant Earthquake Database, including all 5 well-known major Iranian earthquakes (Buin Zahra 1962, Tabas 1978, Manjil-Rudbar 1990, Bam 2003, Kermanshah 2017). No natural-disaster chart concept exists anywhere in CHART_REGISTRY.csv -- entirely new topic for the project.

title: Divorce Rate, Real Gold-Coin Price & Related Covariates: Descriptive Statistics (1980-2014)
notes: AMBIGUOUS / flagged: this is NOT a year-by-year time series -- it's an econometric study's descriptive statistics (mean/min/max/std-dev) for divorce rate, real gold-coin price, female literacy, social globalization index, and GDP-per-capita growth, plus one narrative fact (marriage age rising 24->27, 1986-2011). Registered as its own row because it's the only source in the project linking gold price to divorce rate, but it cannot be charted as a conventional line/bar time series without the underlying Figure 1 data (not text-extractable per this folder's own README) -- a future agent may want to treat this as a footnote/citation rather than a first-class chart.

title: Mean Age at First Marriage by Sex, Urban/Rural/Total (6 Census Years, 1966-2006)
notes: Hajnal's-method mean age at first marriage. No WDI indicator for age-at-marriage exists for Iran. Thematically related to iran_census__marriage_migration_patterns_1956_1991 (staged above, narrower 1956/1991 data points from Encyclopaedia Iranica) -- these two rows should be cross-referenced/considered for a future merge but are kept separate here since they come from different primary sources and cover different year sets.

title: Registered Marriages & Divorces, National Annual (1991-2006) + All Provinces (2006)
notes: Civil Registration Organization data via Iran Data Portal. No WDI marriage/divorce indicator exists for any country in this project's WDI extract. Several years carry a 'Revised figures' footnote flag (1991, 2001, 2005) preserved but not resolved, per the source.

title: Registered Births by Sex, National Annual (1991-2006) + All Provinces (2006)
notes: Civil-registration birth counts by sex, distinct from wdi__SP.DYN.CBRT (a crude birth RATE per 1,000 population, not a sex-disaggregated administrative count) -- not registered as an extension of that chart since the unit and data-generating process differ (registration counts vs. modeled/survey rate).

title: Settled-Urban / Settled-Rural / Unsettled Population & Households by Province (2006 Census)
notes: All 30 provinces + national, sums reconcile exactly to the national 2006 total (70,495,782), independently cross-checked against iran_census_demographics_series/national_and_provincial_summary_2011_census.csv Table 1. WDI has no province or settlement-type breakdown for Iran.

title: Labor Force Participation Rate by Sex, Urban/Rural/Total, Annual (2005-2014)
notes: WDI's SL.TLF.CACT family for Iran starts 1990 and gives no urban/rural split at all -- this national-estimate series (from SCI, not modeled ILO estimates) adds the urban/rural breakdown WDI never carries, for years that otherwise overlap WDI's own coverage.

title: Employed Persons by Occupation Category x Sex x Urban/Rural/Total, Annual (2005-2013)
notes: 891 rows: 11 occupation categories (not economic sectors) x sex x residence, annual. WDI has no occupation-level employment breakdown for Iran (its SL.* indicators are sector- or aggregate-level, never occupation-level) -- genuinely new granularity.

title: Employed Persons by 13 Economic Sectors, Annual (1956-2011)
notes: One of the longest continuous annual series in the whole project (55 years, national level, no urban/rural or sex split). WDI's SL.AGR.EMPL.ZS/SL.IND.EMPL.ZS/SL.SRV.EMPL.ZS (3-sector, modeled ILO estimates) start only 1991 for Iran -- this extends 35 years earlier with actual headcounts at much finer (13-sector) granularity; the 13-way split doesn't decompose cleanly onto WDI's 3 broad sectors, so treat the 'extends' relationship as topical (same underlying question) rather than a literal continuation of the same series. Cross-validated against a 1956/1966 3-sector citation series in iran_census_demographics_series/wb_archives_population_demographic_citations_1956_1982.csv (agriculture/industry/services %-of-active-population rows) -- percentages agree at those two overlapping years; that WB-archives citation was NOT given its own chart_registry row since it duplicates this fuller series at coarser resolution.

title: Employed Persons by Sex & Women's Share of Total Employment, Annual (1966-2011)
notes: Annual headcounts by sex (women's employment share tracked from 13.3% in 1966). WDI's labor-force-by-sex indicators (SL.TLF.CACT.FE/MA.ZS, participation RATES not headcounts) start 1990 -- this extends 24 years earlier, though the unit differs (headcount vs. %). Complements iran_census__wb_archives_labor_force_by_sex_1956_1972 (staged above), which adds a single earlier 1956 data point from an independent WB-archives source.

title: Social Security Organization Pensioners & Amounts Paid, by Category, 8 Years (1991-2006)
notes: Retirees / work-related-disabled / other-cause-disabled / survivors, pensioner counts and rial amounts paid. WDI's per_* (ASPIRE) social-protection indicators are household-survey-based coverage/adequacy percentages, not SSI's own administrative pensioner counts and disbursement amounts -- a fundamentally different data-generating process, genuinely new topic.

title: OPEC Membership, Quota System & Production Policy Milestones for Iran (1960-2025)
notes: Non-continuous dated milestones: 1960 founding membership, 1973 nationalization, 1982 first quota system, 1986 quota collapse, 1990-1993 revisions, 2016/2018 sanctions-era exemption/re-sanctioning, 2020 production trough (1.93M b/d), 2024 recovery. No policy-history chart concept exists in CHART_REGISTRY.csv (which is entirely built from machine-readable statistical series) -- new category for the project. Cross-checked against wdi__EG (energy/oil) and owid__oil_production_volume for the underlying production-volume numbers where they overlap.

title: WTO/GATT Accession Timeline for Iran: Still Incomplete After 3 Decades (1995-2026)
notes: 1996 application, blocked 22 times through 2001-2005, Working Party established 2005, still never held its first meeting as of this database's compilation. Entirely new institutional-history topic, no WDI/FAOSTAT/IMF equivalent.

title: IMF Article IV Consultation History for Iran: Dates & Headline Findings (2002-2025)
notes: 8 completed consultations plus a documented 2019-2024 gap (2018 is the last full consultation; 2025 saw only an informal Board briefing). Distinct from wdi__ and weo__ chart_ids, which are IMF WEO's quantitative macro series, not the qualitative consultation-history record.

title: Parallel-Market Rial/Dollar FX Rate Volatility: Descriptive Statistics (1978-1990)
notes: AMBIGUOUS / flagged, same caveat as the gold-price/divorce-rate study above: this is NOT a month-by-month series, only descriptive statistics (mean, min/max, std-dev, skewness/kurtosis of monthly rate changes) from IMF WP 95/69's Table 1/Chart 1, which were not transcribed into machine-readable form by the extracting agent (source PDF remains in data/raw/ for a future deeper-extraction pass). Notable qualitative facts preserved: parallel-market premium reached 'among the highest ever observed internationally' (~2,000%+ by Dec 1990); 'in certain periods over ten different exchange rates were in effect.' Complements iran_fx__usd_irr_parallel_rate_daily_2011_2026 below (modern daily parallel-rate series) as the pre-2011 narrative predecessor of the same underlying phenomenon.

title: USD/IRR Free-Market (Parallel/Black-Market) Exchange Rate, Daily (2011-11-26 to 2026-07-11)
notes: 3,899 daily observations (open/low/high/close + day-over-day change), parsed from raw TGJU.org JSON. Companion/comparator to wdi__PA.NUS (official DEC/Atlas/PPP conversion factor) but a fundamentally different, unofficial rate -- not registered as an extension of that chart since parallel and official rates are conceptually distinct series (the gap between them is itself the analytically interesting object), not unit variants of the same underlying rate.

title: Bahar Azadi Gold Coin Price, Rial, Daily (2013-07-22 to 2026-07-11)
notes: 3,432 daily observations. Iran's classic inflation-hedge/informal-savings instrument; no gold-coin-price chart concept exists anywhere in the project.

title: Emami Gold Coin Price, Rial, Daily (2010-04-04 to 2026-07-11)
notes: 4,237 daily observations, the longest of the two gold-coin series (starts 3+ years before Bahar Azadi). Same chart-concept family as the Bahar Azadi row above but a genuinely distinct instrument/price series, not a duplicate.

title: Remittances (Aggregate & Bilateral) and Iranian Diaspora Stock by Host Country (2000-2023 / 2021)
notes: Bundles three sub-tables: (1) KNOMAD aggregate remittance in/outflows 2000-2023 (both report an internally-inconsistent exact 0 for Iran every year -- loosely relates to wdi__BX.TRF.PWKR, which does carry some nonzero Iran values, flagging the KNOMAD-vs-WDI discrepancy rather than reconciling it); (2) 2021 bilateral remittance OUTFLOWS from Iran by destination country (Pakistan $53.1M, Afghanistan $123.4M, etc. -- migrant/refugee workers in Iran sending earnings home); (3) 2021 Iranian-diaspora stock by host country (1,387,646 worldwide, largest in the US at 389,383). The bilateral-corridor and diaspora-stock granularity has no WDI equivalent at all (wdi__SM.POP covers migrant stock INTO Iran and net migration, not Iranian emigrant stock abroad by country) -- dominant new content, not set as extends_chart_id despite the loose aggregate-remittance overlap with BX.TRF.PWKR.

title: Non-Carpet Handicraft Exports & Institutional History (1930s-2025)
notes: Non-continuous: export value (COVID collapse $427M->$120M, recovery to ~$400M by 2022/2025), 570,000 registered craftsmen, UNESCO Creative Cities (Isfahan, Bandar Abbas), 1930s Reza-Shah-era craft-preservation schools. State-media-attributed (Tehran Times) per bookkeeping.md rule 4 -- flagged as such, routine trade figures quoted from a named official, not independently cross-checked this round. No FAOSTAT/WDI trade-by-product-category series covers handicrafts.

title: CITES Caviar Export Quota & Trade Timeline (1998-2006)
notes: 1998 baseline exports (40t), 1998-2004 cumulative (480t+, Iran the largest global exporter), the June 2001 Paris Agreement exemption, Caspian-wide quota totals 2001-2003. Per this folder's own README, this 'extends the specialty-goods caviar series' in data/processed/specialty_goods_series/ -- that folder is outside this task's scope and has not itself been registered in CHART_REGISTRY.csv or any chart_registry_staging file yet, so no verifiable extends_chart_id exists to point to; flagged here as a genuine ambiguity for whichever future pass registers specialty_goods_series.

title: Argentina (BCRA) Retail ARS/USD Exchange Rate, Daily (2010-2026)
notes: Raw BCRA central-bank API data, JSON-to-CSV conversion only, no independent curation beyond that (per this project's Iran-first priority). No Argentina FX comparator chart exists yet in CHART_REGISTRY.csv (which is Iran-only, built purely from Iran rows of the WDI/FAOSTAT/WEO/OWID/Maddison/WID extracts) -- this is a new comparator-chart CATEGORY for the project, not an extension of any Iran chart.
